destinations.evidence: The DKMS request codec decodes the eight ordered fields and offers efficient executor/TTL extraction through the shared ExecutorRequest base fields.
Detector/RPC validation paths consume the DKMS codec and enforce the decoded base fields before DKMS-specific owner validation.

equivalence_argument: The DKMS ABI is the current eight-field base-plus-DKMS shape: executor/encrypted_secrets/ttl/secret_signature/user_public_key/owner/key_index/key_format, with width checks and shared-base executor/TTL extraction.
notes: This is the current DKMS behavior and supersedes the older five-field no-secrets record.
rationale: The primary diff replaces the old five-field DKMS ABI with the shared executor precompile base plus DKMS fields and updates async detector/validation paths to extract executor and TTL through DkmsKeyRequest::extract_executor_and_ttl. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: DkmsKeyRequest ABI decoding and encoding must use eight fields in order: executor, encrypted_secrets, ttl, secret_signature, user_public_key, owner, key_index, key_format; ttl and key_index must fit in u64, key_format must fit in u8, and efficient executor/ttl extraction must decode the shared ExecutorRequest base fields.
suggested_falsification_tests: Encode a DKMS key request with non-empty base ExecutorRequest arrays and DKMS owner/key fields, assert from_abi round-trips all eight fields and extract_executor_and_ttl returns the same executor and ttl; assert oversized ttl/key_index or key_format fail instead of truncating.
summary: DKMS key requests include the shared ExecutorRequest base.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Encode a DKMS key request with non-empty base ExecutorRequest arrays and DKMS owner/key fields, assert from_abi round-trips all eight fields and extract_executor_and_ttl returns the same executor and ttl; assert oversized ttl/key_index or key_format fail instead of truncating.
title: DKMS key requests include ExecutorRequest base
